rulings.Case Title: BRYNN K. STOLL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Employee Bryant Woo
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF BRYNN STOLL's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Employee Bryant Woo. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to Appear. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: UNILOC USA, INC. VS. ALAN HAHN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of His Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Form Interrogatories; And Requests For Admission
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2015, Line 5, RESPONDENT ALAN HAHN's Motion In Support Of His Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Form Interrogatories; And Requests For Admission. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Having read and considered the parties' briefs and supporting papers, the motion is granted in part. The parties have resolved issues relating to Respondent Hahn's Requests for Admissions and Form Interrogatories. Appellant Uniloc U.S.A., Inc. is ordered to produce verified supplemental responses and documents responsive to Respondent's Request for Production Nos. 1, 4, 5, 6, 9, and 13, within five (5) business days. Consistent with the parties' agreement, Appellant Uniloc U.S.A., Inc. is further ordered to produce verified supplemental responses and documents responsive to Respondent's Request for Production Nos. 18, 20, 23, and 25, with individual names redacted, within five (5) business days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to kathyhuibonhoa@gbgllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: L & C INVESTMENT GROUP, VS. PETER QI QIAO CHOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF L & C INVESTMENT GROUP's Motion To Compel Deposition And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Having read and considered the parties' briefs and supporting papers, including defendants' statement they do not object to entry of an Order that they appear for deposition, the motion is granted. Defendants are ordered to appear for deposition on April 8 or 9, 2015; if neither date is agreeable to Plaintiff, the deposition(s) shall occur on a date thereafter that is mutually agreeable to the parties. Plaintiff's request for sanctions under CCP section 2025.450(c)(1) is denied, as the Court finds that the parties were engaged in good faith efforts to schedule the depositions, but Plaintiff filed the instant motion before that process completed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to kathyhuibonhoa@gbgllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FOND EXPRESS INTERNATIONAL(SFO), INC. VS. HII FINANCE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 9, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT HII FINANCE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Granted. A plaintiff opposing a motion to quash has the initial burden of demonstrating facts establishing a basis for personal jurisdiction. (Snowney v. Harrah's Entertainment, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1054, 1062.) Plaintiff has not met its initial burden. The only piece of admissible evidence, Exhibit A to the Declaration of Lawrence Lee, does not establish that Defendant had the requisite minimum contacts with California to permit jurisdiction over it. The email is signed by "Hassan Judeh on behalf of ISDA and Co." and does not say anything about Defendant being responsible for ISDA's contracts or debt. Further, the email does not say anything about Defendant controlling or operating ISDA. Defendant's objection to Exhibit A to the Declaration of Lawrence Lee (objection no. 4) is overruled. All of Defendant's other objections (objections nos. 1-3, 5) are sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)
